ai_agent.role: Manages the full permit lifecycle: document collection, county submission, revision tracking, issuance, builders risk, NOC, modifications and extensions. Handles county-specific requirements for Marion, Citrus, Charlotte, Sarasota, and Lake counties.
workflow_chains: first_draw_received -> request_survey -> request_floor_plan -> request_energy_calc -> request_septic -> request_trusses -> docs_complete -> apply_permit
permit_applied -> track_revisions -> ready_to_issue -> notify_financeiro -> payment_confirmed -> print_and_send_to_field
permit_issued -> builders_risk_upload -> builders_risk_payment -> noc_preparation -> client_signature -> noc_filed

staff.name: Cassia Silva (Cacia)
staff.role: Permits Leader - overall permit management, contract creation, builders risk control, printing oversight, NOC preparation, custom home permit assignments, field document delivery

staff.name: Kaio Vinicius
staff.role: Permits Coordinator - document requests and follow-up, county submissions, revision tracking, status updates, application execution for both spec and custom homes

staff.name: Andreia
staff.role: Printing - responsible for printing approved permit documents with county stamps for field delivery

core_knowledge.description: The mandatory document collection sequence for spec homes is always: 1) Survey (prerequisite for all others), 2) Floor Plan (~5 days), 3) Energy Calculations (same day), 4) Septic (~7 days), 5) Trusses (uses pilot/model file). Survey must be received before requesting floor plan, energy calc, and septic.

Permit tracking spreadsheet uses color codes: RED = document requested/awaiting, GREEN = document received/ready to issue, YELLOW = model change requiring document update, BLUE = hold/impediment blocking progress, PINK = permit approved and issued, WHITE = printed and sent to field.

All counties require: Floor Plan, Energy Calculations, Septic Document, Trusses, Survey, NOC, Product Approval List. Additional requirements vary: Marion County needs Application Form; Citrus County needs Application Forms + Civic Application; Sarasota needs Drainage Plan + Application Form. Citrus has ~40% waiver rate for lot height issues and recurring Arrows (drainage signage) problems.

Permits are generally valid for 6 months (condominiums: 1 year). Expiration date resets automatically with each scheduled inspection. If permit expires 2+ times, risk of complete cancellation. Florida Section 553.79(14): permit expires if no inspection activity in 180 days. Marion County no longer sends email warnings for expiration.

Builders Risk insurance can ONLY be filed AFTER permit payment is confirmed by Financeiro. NOC (Notice of Commencement) requires client signature and is a meticulous document with full project data. System must block builders risk advancement without confirmed financial payment.

Custom homes have no standardized permit stages. May require additional documents: wetland determination, HOA approval, demolition permit, well permits, septic-specific documentation. Pre-agreement status authorized by Bernardo. Knowledge concentrated in Kaio and Cassia - high risk if either leaves.

Marion County permits have a strict 6-month validity window. Team no longer receives email warnings about expiration - they discover it only when trying to schedule an inspection. This is a critical operational risk.

Approximately 40% of homes in Citrus County require a Waiver due to lot height being below minimum county requirements. The Arrows issue (drainage signage documentation) is also a recurrent problem causing delays. Both require formal county submissions.

core_knowledge.topic: Permit Revision Stages in Const App
core_knowledge.description: The permit revision process follows 6 stages: 1) 91 Review, 2) Building, 3) Zoning, 4) Fee, 5) NOC, 6) Builder's Risk. Each stage must be tracked and completed sequentially.

daily_workflows.name: Document Request and Follow-up
steps: Check permit spreadsheet for new houses triggered by first draw
Identify house number, address, county, and project folder
Request Survey from surveyor (Anel or Kaio) via email with address and parcel ID
Upon survey receipt, request Floor Plan, Energy Calculations, Septic, and Trusses from respective subs
Update color-coded status for each document (red=requested, green=received)
When all documents received, mark house as green (ready to apply)
critical_decisions: Whether to request documents in parallel or wait for survey first
Whether a waiver is needed (Citrus County lot height assessment)
Whether custom home requires non-standard documents

daily_workflows.name: County Submission and Tracking
steps: Verify all documents complete and received
Create submission folder: Energy, Floor Plan, Site Plan, Survey + county-specific docs
Submit permit application to county via email
Record Permit Number received from county
Move house from application table to county-specific table
Monitor status: pending, approved, denied via county portal
critical_decisions: Whether submission is complete for the specific county
Whether denied status requires corrective action or resubmission

daily_workflows.name: Permit Issuance and Field Delivery
steps: Identify permits with 'Ready to Issue' status
Notify Financeiro (Cintia) for permit payment on county site
Confirm payment received from Financeiro
Mark house as pink (approved) in spreadsheet
Andreia prints documents with county stamps
Deliver printed folder to Cassia
Cassia sends approved documents to the field
Mark as white (printed/sent) in spreadsheet
critical_decisions: Verify only stamped (county-sealed) versions go to field
Confirm builders risk cannot proceed without payment confirmation

daily_workflows.name: Daily Log Documentation
steps: Record document requests and receipts from subs
Log permit applications and status changes
Document revision cases and hold situations
Tag entries with standardized permit tags (#P - PERMIT APPLIED, #P - SURVEY CHECKLIST DONE, etc.)

features_used: Permit Management - track permits with stages (Not Started, Applied, Issued, Canceled)
Permit Application - submit and monitor application documents (Energy Calc, Floor Plan, NOC, Survey)
Permit Revision - monitor county review process (91 Review, Building, Zoning, Fee, NOC, Builder's Risk)
Documentation - manage document stamps and field packets (PRE-PERMIT, APPROVED, MISCELLANEOUS)
Modifications & Extensions - handle permit changes after issuance
api_endpoints: /permits - GET list of permits, POST create new permit

handoffs.trigger: Floor Plan Complete - all pre-permit documents received and house marked green
handoffs.data_exchanged: Floor plan PDF, survey documents, energy calculations, model specifications
handoffs.impact: Critical path - without floor plan, takeoff cannot begin; without takeoff, budget cannot be created; without budget, scheduling is blocked

handoffs.trigger: Permit status changes to 'Ready to Issue'
handoffs.data_exchanged: Permit number, county portal link, payment amount for permit release fee
handoffs.impact: Payment must be made before permit can be issued. Builders Risk filing blocked until payment confirmed.

handoffs.data_exchanged: Payment confirmation, receipt from county portal
handoffs.impact: Enables builders risk filing and permit issuance to field

handoffs.trigger: Floor plan and project data ready from Design Office
handoffs.data_exchanged: Floor plan PDF, project specifications, model type, county assignment
handoffs.impact: Cannot begin permit document collection without floor plan

handoffs.trigger: Septic vs gravity system determination affects permit documentation needs
handoffs.data_exchanged: Water/sewer system type (septic vs gravity), utility availability letter (UAL), permit number
handoffs.impact: Septic system requires DOH inspection and specific documentation in permit package

handoffs.trigger: NOC requires client signature; CR requests permit status updates
handoffs.data_exchanged: NOC document for signature, permit status for client communication
handoffs.impact: NOC cannot be filed without client signature; CR needs permit timeline for client expectations

handoffs.trigger: Permit approved and printed with county stamps
handoffs.data_exchanged: Physical folder with stamped approved documents
handoffs.impact: Construction cannot begin without stamped permit documents on site (mandatory in Citrus County)

pain_points.description: No email warning for permit expiration from Marion County. Team discovers expiration only when attempting to schedule an inspection, creating risk of permit cancellation if expired 2+ times.
pain_points.current_workaround: Manual checking of county portal periodically; team discovers expiration 'by surprise'
pain_points.proposed_solution: Automated monitoring of permit expiration dates with alerts at 30, 15, and 7 days before expiration. System should track last inspection date and calculate remaining validity window.

pain_points.description: Wrong document versions reaching field. No version control system means outdated floor plans or permit documents may be sent to construction supervisors, causing building errors.
pain_points.current_workaround: Manual file management with 'revision' folders in Google Drive; relies on team diligence
pain_points.proposed_solution: Automated document versioning with mandatory supersede workflow. Only latest stamped version accessible to field. Alert when document is updated that has already been sent to field.

pain_points.description: Permit modifications and extensions are managed entirely manually with no standardized tracking or deadline alerts.
pain_points.current_workaround: Spreadsheet notes and manual email follow-up with county
pain_points.proposed_solution: Structured modification/extension tracking with automated deadline reminders and county submission templates

pain_points.description: 16+ POs and daily logs duplicated for same owner in condominiums, creating excessive administrative burden.
pain_points.current_workaround: Manual duplication of entries across all units
pain_points.proposed_solution: Batch entry support for multi-unit projects with single owner

pain_points.description: Communication of hold status is not centralized for the entire company. Different departments may not know a house is on hold.
pain_points.current_workaround: Manual notification via email and Slack to affected parties
pain_points.proposed_solution: Centralized hold status visible across all department dashboards with automatic notification

pain_points.description: Custom home permit process has no standardization. Each project may require unique documents that are not predictable in advance.
pain_points.current_workaround: Cassia and Kaio manage ad hoc based on experience; knowledge concentrated in two people
pain_points.proposed_solution: Custom home permit checklist builder with library of possible requirements per county, with ability to add non-standard items

pain_points.description: Waivers and Arrows in Citrus County generate recurrent rework affecting ~40% of houses.
pain_points.current_workaround: Kaio handles each waiver individually; knows the process but it is not documented
pain_points.proposed_solution: Automated waiver detection based on lot data (height check), with pre-filled waiver application templates

tribal_knowledge.description: Each county (Marion, Citrus, Charlotte, Sarasota, Lake) has distinct submission requirements, revision stages, portal interfaces, and timing expectations. Marion County does not require permit before site work (can proceed until Form). Citrus County requires permit BEFORE any construction begins. Knowledge of which inspectors to contact, portal navigation, and common rejection reasons is held by Kaio and Cassia.
tribal_knowledge.risk_if_lost: Critical - new staff would face weeks of learning curve per county; risk of submission errors, rejections, and construction delays

tribal_knowledge.description: The process for requesting lot height waivers in Citrus County (~40% of houses), including the correct forms, measurement methodology, supporting documentation, and county reviewer preferences. Also includes knowledge of Arrows (drainage signage) requirements and common submission pitfalls.
tribal_knowledge.risk_if_lost: High - waiver rejections would delay 40% of Citrus County projects; process is undocumented

tribal_knowledge.description: Expected turnaround times by county and document type: survey (immediate), energy calc (same day), floor plan (~5 days), septic (~7 days). Knowledge of when counties are slow (holidays, staff changes), which reviewers are thorough vs lenient, and optimal submission timing to avoid backlogs.
tribal_knowledge.risk_if_lost: Medium - suboptimal timing would lead to longer permit cycles and missed windows

tribal_knowledge.description: Preferred surveyor contacts (Anel, AMB Surveying), energy calculation providers (Quick eCalcs at $200), floor plan architects (Gizelle Holz & Eliane at $750), septic consultants (Rapid Septic Consulting at $600), truss engineers (Truss Direct LLC). Includes knowledge of each sub's reliability, turnaround, and pricing.
tribal_knowledge.risk_if_lost: Medium - would need to rebuild vendor relationships and negotiate new pricing

topics: Understanding the permit lifecycle: application through issuance
Document sequence for spec homes (Survey -> Floor Plan -> Energy Calc -> Septic -> Trusses)
Color code system for permit tracking
County portal navigation (Accela for Marion and Citrus)
Permit validity rules: 6-month window, inspection resets, expiration risks
training_curriculum.quiz_coverage: Permit stages, color codes, document sequence, county requirements, expiration rules

topics: Marion County: application form, no pre-construction permit requirement, 6-month window
Citrus County: civic application, waiver process (~40%), Arrows requirements, permit required before construction
Common rejection reasons and resubmission workflow by county
training_curriculum.quiz_coverage: County-specific document requirements, waiver triggers, rejection handling

topics: Builders Risk filing process and payment dependency
NOC preparation and client signature requirements
Financial integration: payment confirmation before builders risk
Daily log tagging for permit milestones
training_curriculum.quiz_coverage: Builders risk prerequisites, NOC workflow, daily log tags

topics: Special requirements: HOA approval, demolition permits, wetland determination
training_curriculum.quiz_coverage: Custom home special requirements, pre-agreement flow, document variability

compliance_requirements.requirement: Active permit must be on-site before construction begins in Citrus County
compliance_requirements.verification: Verify stamped permit documents delivered to field supervisor before any construction activity

compliance_requirements.requirement: Permit expires if no inspection scheduled within 180 days
compliance_requirements.verification: Track last inspection date per permit; alert at 150 days with no activity

compliance_requirements.requirement: NOC must be filed with client signature before construction begins
compliance_requirements.verification: Signed NOC document on file before first construction activity

compliance_requirements.requirement: Builders Risk insurance filed only after permit payment confirmation
compliance_requirements.regulation: Internal policy - insurance validity requires active permit
compliance_requirements.verification: System blocks builders risk filing without Financeiro payment confirmation

compliance_requirements.requirement: Only county-stamped document versions accessible to field personnel
compliance_requirements.verification: Field folder contains only documents with official county stamp/seal

key_metrics.metric: Average permit cycle time (application to issuance)
key_metrics.target: < 7 weeks for spec homes, < 12 weeks for custom
key_metrics.measurement: Days from first draw notification to permit issued date

key_metrics.target: All 5 core documents within 10 business days of first draw
key_metrics.measurement: Days from first draw to all documents marked green
Number of rejected submissions / total submissions per quarter
Count of permits that expired due to missed monitoring

key_metrics.target: < 2 business days from payment confirmation to field delivery
key_metrics.measurement: Days from Financeiro payment to physical folder sent to field
